title: olea europaea subsp. cerasiformis
intro: Olea europaea subsp. cerasiformis, commonly known as wild olive or dwarf olive, is a subspecies of the olive tree (Olea europaea) native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The leaves, bark, and fruit of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Olea europaea subsp. cerasiformis include reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
